summary: The AC6 wording now correctly narrows the scope guard to the three substantive cleanup files plus workflow artifacts, and no product/doc cleanup files changed after 35bd2cc. However, the approved evidence fix is not complete: the recorded diff-stat evidence is still not the actual command output, and the story status was changed to merged while the branch is still unmerged locally.

findings.title: Diff-stat evidence is still inaccurate
findings.claim: The build note still labels the block as `git diff --stat main...HEAD`, but it is not the actual output: the real stat includes five files, exact counts for `reviews/backlog-cleanup.codex.json` and `reviews/backlog-cleanup.md`, and the `5 files changed, 153 insertions(+), 28 deletions(-)` total. The re-review note also records `reviews/backlog-cleanup.md | 63 ...`, while actual `git diff --stat 35bd2cc...HEAD` reports `reviews/backlog-cleanup.md | 74 ...` and `2 files changed, 64 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)`. Since AC6 is proven by this evidence, the approved diff-stat fix remains incomplete.
findings.suggestion: Paste the exact current `git diff --stat main...HEAD` output, and if the re-review stat is kept, paste the exact `git diff --stat 35bd2cc...HEAD` output. Put the workflow-artifact explanation outside the stat block.

findings.title: Story status was changed to merged before merge
findings.claim: The diff changes the story status from `approved` to `merged`, but this branch is still ahead of `main` locally and HEAD is not an ancestor of `main`. The only approved fix was the build-note evidence correction, so this adds an inaccurate workflow-state change outside the approved fix.
findings.suggestion: Leave the status as the pre-merge review state until the branch is actually merged, then update it to `merged` in the merge/finalization step.
